name: Monday_Work_Manager
description: Manages work items, boards, and projects in Monday.com as the Mind's external execution layer. Creates, organizes, and tracks project work. Bridges cognitive intent from the Mind's WORK surface to actionable items in Monday.com boards.
progressive_unlock: Board and item reading available immediately. Item creation and updates unlock after Mind has retrieved board items at least once to understand board structure and available columns. Search functionality available once at least two boards have been accessed.
constraints: Never expose Monday.com API token in logs or outputs
Respect Monday.com GraphQL rate limit of 5,000,000 complexity points per minute
Track GraphQL query complexity using complexity_estimate values from tool schemas. Halt execution if remaining complexity budget falls below 500 points.
Do not create or update items without explicit Steward confirmation unless the Mind has standing permission in TENET
Sync item state with WORK surface — Monday.com items should mirror WORK board cards where applicable
Do not create duplicate items — always search for existing items via Monday_GetBoardItems or Monday_SearchItems before creation
column_values parameter must be passed as JSON string, not direct object. Use 'JSON.stringify()' for proper formatting.
Store active board IDs and column schemas in STREAM.entity for quick access and reuse
Group IDs are board-specific — cache group listings per board in STREAM to minimize API calls
When searching items, prefer specific column searches over broad queries to reduce complexity costs

steps.instruction: Validate input against Layer 3 Tool Schema. Confirm required parameters present and correctly typed. Verify JSON string formatting for column_values.
Retrieve Monday.com API token from TENET.apiKey.monday. Construct Authorization header as 'Bearer {token}' for GraphQL POST request.
Determine intent: review (list boards/items/groups), search (find items), create (new item), or update (modify item). Check STREAM.entity for cached board/column data.
For board review: fetch Monday_ListBoards with optional name/state filters. Store returned board IDs and metadata in STREAM.entity. If board count exceeds 25, use pagination.

For item retrieval: call Monday_GetBoardItems with board_id. Cache column schema and group IDs in STREAM.entity. Cross-reference with WORK surface cards for consistency. Return item data with all column values.
For group listing: call Monday_ListGroups to retrieve group structure for a board. Cache group hierarchy in STREAM.entity for item creation operations.

For item search: call Monday_SearchItems with column_id and search_value. If boards parameter not provided, search only active boards from STREAM cache to reduce complexity. Return matched items with board context.

For item creation: check existing items first via Monday_GetBoardItems or Monday_SearchItems to prevent duplicates. Request Steward confirmation before execution. Format column_values as JSON string. Execute Monday_CreateItem. Track GraphQL complexity used. If item maps to a WORK card, store the Monday.com item_id in the card's data field.

For item updates: fetch current item state first via Monday_GetBoardItems. Request Steward confirmation before execution. Format column_values changes as JSON string. Execute Monday_UpdateItem. Track GraphQL complexity used. If a WORK card exists for this item, update its status to match.

After each operation, validate GraphQL complexity tracking. Log complexity_estimate + actual overhead (estimated 10-15% per query). If complexity budget drops below 500 points, pause operations and alert Steward.

Compile work summary with active boards, item counts, group structure, and recent updates. Push daily work summary to LTM with key: 'monday_daily_{date}'. Update STREAM with active item counts and board status.
